bib.title: Detecting policy conflicts by model checking UML state machines
bib.citation: Feature Interactions in Software and Communication Systems X, 59-74, 2009
bib.author: Maurice H Ter Beek and Stefania Gnesi and Carlo Montangero and Laura Semini
bib.abstract: Policies are convenient means to modify system behaviour at run-time. Nowadays, policies are created in great numbers by different actors, ranging from system administrators to lay-users. However, this situation may lead naturally to inconsistencies, a problem that has been recognized and termed policy conflict.
